# UC-17.1.11 · Posture Assessment Failure Trends

## Description

Time-series view of posture failures by policy and reason — distinguishes one-off issues from worsening fleet hygiene or a bad policy rollout.

## Value

Time-series view of posture failures by policy and reason — distinguishes one-off issues from worsening fleet hygiene or a bad policy rollout.

## Implementation

Normalize `failure_reason` from ISE. Alert when daily failures exceed 7-day baseline by >50%. Segment by AD group or location if extracted.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk_TA_cisco-ise.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: ISE posture assessment logs (`cisco:ise:posture`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize `failure_reason` from ISE. Alert when daily failures exceed 7-day baseline by >50%. Segment by AD group or location if extracted.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:posture" earliest=-30d
| where posture_status="NonCompliant"
| timechart span=1d count by failure_reason
```

Understanding this SPL

**Posture Assessment Failure Trends** — Time-series view of posture failures by policy and reason — distinguishes one-off issues from worsening fleet hygiene or a bad policy rollout.

Documented **Data sources**: ISE posture assessment logs (`cisco:ise:posture`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk_TA_cisco-ise. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:posture. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:posture", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where posture_status="NonCompliant"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by failure_reason** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (failures over time by reason), Stacked area (failures by policy), Single value (failures vs prior week).

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:posture" earliest=-30d
| where posture_status="NonCompliant"
| timechart span=1d count by failure_reason
```

## Visualization

Line chart (failures over time by reason), Stacked area (failures by policy), Single value (failures vs prior week).
